description.task: Create a ServiceBoundaryAnalyzer class that models a microservices domain decomposition. Given a list of business capabilities, group them into cohesive services based on shared domain context and report the suggested service breakdown.
instructions: addCapability() uses computeIfAbsent to append the capability to the named service's list
capabilitiesOf() returns the list for the service, or an empty list if the service name is unknown
violatesSingleResponsibility() returns true when the service has strictly more than maxCapabilities
serviceCount() returns the total number of distinct service names registered
The order of capabilities must match the order they were added
description.hint: Use serviceCapabilities.getOrDefault(serviceName, List.of()) in capabilitiesOf(). For violatesSingleResponsibility(), compare capabilitiesOf(serviceName).size() > maxCapabilities.
description.expectedOutput:
Services: 2
UserService capabilities: [register, login, updateProfile]
Violates SRP (max 2): true

description.task: Implement a ServiceRegistry class that models a microservices service discovery mechanism (like Eureka). Services register with their host/port, can be deregistered, and clients can discover healthy instances of a service.
instructions: register() appends a new ServiceInstance with healthy=true to the service's list
deregister() removes the first instance matching both host and port; returns true if found
discover() returns only instances where healthy=true
discover() returns an empty list for unknown service names
Multiple instances of the same service can coexist (load balancing scenario)
description.hint: For deregister(), use removeIf() on the list: list.removeIf(i -> i.host().equals(host) && i.port() == port). Track whether any element was actually removed.
description.expectedOutput:
Payment instances: 2
Removed: true
Payment instances after: 1

description.task: Implement a CircuitBreaker class that protects microservices from cascading failures. The circuit breaker transitions between CLOSED (normal), OPEN (blocking calls), and HALF_OPEN (trial calls) states based on failure thresholds and recovery attempts.
instructions: recordFailure() increments failureCount; when failureCount >= failureThreshold, set state=OPEN
recordSuccess() only acts when state is HALF_OPEN: increment successCount, set CLOSED when successCount >= successThreshold
recordSuccess() in CLOSED state resets failureCount to 0
attemptReset() transitions state from OPEN to HALF_OPEN and resets successCount to 0
allowRequest() returns true for CLOSED and HALF_OPEN, false for OPEN
description.hint: In recordFailure(), after incrementing, check if failureCount >= failureThreshold and set state = State.OPEN. In attemptReset(), only change state if currently OPEN.
description.expectedOutput:
After 3 failures: OPEN
After reset attempt: HALF_OPEN
After 2 successes: CLOSED
